---
title: "Survey & Instrument Design"
---

::: {.topic-badges}
[Moderate]{.level-badge .level-moderate} · **Methods & Study Design** track · ~35 min hands-on + readings and quiz
:::

[← Back to the Methods & Study Design track](methods-study-design.qmd){.track-back}

*Use AI to draft and critique survey items — while guarding against bias and bad measurement.*

## What you'll be able to do

- Draft survey items, scales, and skip logic with AI
- Critique items for bias, ambiguity, and double-barrels
- Plan cognitive testing and a codebook

## Overview

AI can draft candidate items, suggest response scales, and write cognitive-interview probes. It can also produce leading or double-barreled items, so you critique every one against measurement principles.

Good instruments are validated, not just written. AI accelerates drafting; piloting and cognitive testing with real respondents still decide what works.

## Practice activities

```{=html}
<details class="activity"><summary>Activity 1 · Moderate — Draft and critique items</summary>
```

**Time** ~18 min · **Tools** ChatGPT Edu

**Goal.** Generate candidate items, then pressure-test them.

**Setup.** A construct to measure — e.g., *perceived pedestrian safety in your neighborhood.*

**Steps.**

1. Draft items:

   > Draft 6 candidate survey items measuring [construct], with a response scale. Keep them concrete and answerable. [construct]

2. Critique them:

   > Critique each item for bias, ambiguity, double-barreling, and reading level.

3. Cut or fix based on the critique — your judgment, not the model’s.

**Expected result.** A vetted short item set with a response scale.

**Check your work.** Does each item measure the construct, or something adjacent?

**Common pitfalls.** AI produces leading and double-barreled items confidently. You own measurement quality.

**Stretch (optional).** Ask for two items at different reading levels and keep the clearer.

```{=html}
</details>
```

```{=html}
<details class="activity"><summary>Activity 2 · Moderate — Write cognitive-interview probes</summary>
```

**Time** ~12 min · **Tools** ChatGPT Edu

**Goal.** Plan to test whether respondents understand your items.

**Setup.** Two of your items.

**Steps.**

1. Draft probes:

   > Write cognitive-interview probes for these two items (comprehension, retrieval, judgment). [items]

2. Decide which probes you’d actually use.

3. Predict failure:

   > What’s the most likely way a respondent could misunderstand item 1?

**Expected result.** Usable probes and a predicted misunderstanding.

**Check your work.** Real piloting validates items — drafting doesn’t.

**Common pitfalls.** Skipping cognitive testing ships ambiguous items. AI helps draft, not validate.

**Stretch (optional).** Plan a 3-person cognitive pretest.

```{=html}
</details>
```


## Check your readiness

Answer these, then check — your score suggests whether to dive in or skim the readings first.

<div class="readiness-quiz">
<script type="application/json" class="quiz-data">
{"questions": [{"q": "AI-drafted survey items must be checked for…", "options": ["Bias, ambiguity, and double-barreling", "Spelling only", "Length only", "Font"], "answer": 0, "explain": "Measurement quality is the point."}, {"q": "What validates an instrument?", "options": ["Piloting and cognitive testing with respondents", "A fluent AI draft", "A long item list", "A big model"], "answer": 0, "explain": "Real testing, not drafting, validates."}, {"q": "AI's role in instrument design is to…", "options": ["Accelerate drafting and critique", "Replace validation", "Pick the sample", "Run the study"], "answer": 0, "explain": "It assists; you validate."}]}
</script>
</div>

## Recommended readings

Available in the shared OneDrive folder **Staff Faculty AI Workshop → Readings**, and online where linked:

- [**AI and Survey Sampling Problems (Sharon Lohr)**](https://www.sharonlohr.com/blog/2026/6/10/ai-and-sampling-problems) — sampling and representativeness cautions.
- [**A Framework for Technical Writing in the Age of LLMs**](https://thedataquarry.com/blog/a-framework-for-reading-and-writing/) — precision in wording.

## Useful resources

- [ChatGPT Edu (Drexel AI Tools)](https://drexel.edu/provost/ai/tools) — the approved tool, with data rules.

---

## Navigate

**Next in this track:** [Program Evaluation](program-evaluation.qmd)

**Related topics:** [Program Evaluation](program-evaluation.qmd) · [Qualitative Data Analysis](qualitative-analysis.qmd)

::: {.callout-warning}
## Data rules
Draft with **non-sensitive** content; respondent data later requires an approved tool. See [Tools & Access](../tools.qmd) and Drexel's [AI Tools page](https://drexel.edu/provost/ai/tools).
:::
